_comment: SANITIZED example profile. Copy to profile.json (gitignored) and put your real details there.
No phone, no email, no street address — this file is public.
location_preference is a RANKING signal, never a filter. The pipeline surfaces every US job
and uses these tiers to decide what floats to the top. Filtering on them would hide the jobs
headline: Senior Data & AI Platform Engineer

location_preference._comment: Lower tier = better. The pipeline ranks by this; it never drops on it.

tiers.name: target_metro_hybrid
tiers.why: Would relocate immediately. Hybrid in the target metro.

tiers.name: target_metro_onsite
tiers.why: Would relocate immediately. On-site in the target county.

tiers.name: hybrid_rare_travel
tiers.why: Anywhere in the US, if the office is needed no more than ~twice a month.

tiers.name: current_base_hybrid
tiers.why: Acceptable as a temporary bridge (6-12 months) to gain experience, then relocate.

target_metro._comment: Where you want to end up. Cities are matched against the job's location text.

skills._comment: have = shipped, and safe to claim. building = in progress; surface the job, but say so honestly.

skip_flags._comment: Hard disqualifiers. Encoded in Phase 2 scoring; never guessed at by the prefilter.

salary._comment: NO salary filter, deliberately. Salary is shown as information; it never excludes a job. An estimated salary is never treated as fact.

domains._comment: Core = his home turf, weigh strongly. Secondary = a real plus when it appears (rare), never a filter and never inflate a weak skills match on its own.
secondary: sports / sports-betting / prediction markets (he holds an MS in Sports Management and a BS in Basketball Coaching, and is happy to do backend engineering in this space)
